name: GPD Physics Benchmark — Condensed Matter
description: Benchmark tasks from condensed matter physics: band theory, many-body physics, topological phases, and response functions.

tasks.title: Bloch theorem and band structure of a 1D periodic potential
tasks.description: Derive Bloch's theorem for a particle in a 1D periodic potential and explain the origin of band gaps.
tasks.problem_statement: For a particle in a one-dimensional periodic potential V(x) = V(x + a), prove that the energy eigenstates can be written as psi_k(x) = e^{ikx} u_k(x) where u_k(x + a) = u_k(x). Then, using the nearly-free electron model with a weak periodic potential V(x) = 2V_0 cos(2 pi x / a), derive the energy gap at the first Brillouin zone boundary k = pi/a.
given: Translation operator: T_a psi(x) = psi(x + a)
Nearly-free electron: V(x) = 2V_0 cos(2 pi x / a) with V_0 << E_F
assumptions: Periodic boundary conditions on the crystal
tasks.expected_answer: Bloch's theorem: psi_k(x) = e^{ikx} u_k(x). Energy gap at zone boundary: Delta E = 2|V_0|.
verification_hints: Bloch's theorem follows from [H, T_a] = 0 and the eigenvalues of T_a being e^{ika}
The gap arises from degenerate perturbation theory at the zone boundary
For V_0 -> 0, recover free electron parabola E = hbar^2 k^2 / (2m)
The gap opens where the Bragg condition is satisfied: 2k = 2pi n/a

tasks.title: Berry phase of a two-level system
tasks.description: Compute the Berry phase acquired by the ground state of a spin-1/2 particle in a slowly rotating magnetic field.
tasks.problem_statement: A spin-1/2 particle is placed in a magnetic field B that slowly traces a closed loop on the sphere, subtending solid angle Omega. Compute the Berry phase gamma acquired by the ground state |down> (spin anti-parallel to B) after one complete cycle.
given: Hamiltonian: H = -mu B dot sigma / 2 where sigma are Pauli matrices
B = B_0 (sin(theta) cos(phi), sin(theta) sin(phi), cos(theta))
The field direction (theta, phi) traces a closed loop on the unit sphere
assumptions: Adiabatic evolution (field changes slowly compared to energy gap)
conventions: Berry phase: gamma_n = oint A_n dot dR
tasks.expected_answer: gamma = -Omega/2 where Omega is the solid angle subtended by the loop on the unit sphere.
verification_hints: For a full sphere (Omega = 4 pi): gamma = -2 pi, which is equivalent to gamma = 0 mod 2pi
For a half sphere (Omega = 2 pi): gamma = -pi, giving a sign change
The Berry curvature is that of a magnetic monopole in parameter space
This is the Aharonov-Bohm effect in parameter space: Berry curvature = (1/2) sin(theta) in spherical coordinates

reference.title: Quantal Phase Factors Accompanying Adiabatic Changes

tasks.title: Lindhard function for free electrons
tasks.description: Derive the static Lindhard dielectric function for a free electron gas in 3D.
tasks.problem_statement: Derive the static (omega = 0) Lindhard dielectric function epsilon(q, 0) for a three-dimensional free electron gas at zero temperature. Show that in the long-wavelength limit (q -> 0) it reduces to the Thomas-Fermi result.
given: Free electron gas with Fermi wavevector k_F and density n
Bare Coulomb interaction: v(q) = 4 pi e^2 / q^2
conventions: Hartree atomic units or SI with explicit factors
tasks.expected_answer: epsilon(q, 0) = 1 + (k_TF^2 / q^2) * F(q/(2k_F)) where F(x) = (1/2) + (1 - x^2)/(4x) * ln|(1+x)/(1-x)| and k_TF^2 = 4 k_F / (pi a_0) is the Thomas-Fermi wavevector.
verification_hints: At q -> 0: F(0) = 1, recovering epsilon = 1 + k_TF^2/q^2 (Thomas-Fermi)
At q = 2k_F: the derivative of F has a logarithmic singularity (Kohn anomaly)
This singularity causes Friedel oscillations in real space: V(r) ~ cos(2k_F r)/r^3
For large q >> 2k_F: epsilon -> 1 (no screening at short wavelengths)

reference.title: Quantum Theory of the Electron Liquid

tasks.title: Quantized Hall conductance from Kubo formula
tasks.description: Show that the Hall conductance of a filled Landau level is quantized to e^2/h using the Kubo formula.
tasks.problem_statement: Using the Kubo formula for the Hall conductivity, show that sigma_{xy} for a fully filled band in a 2D periodic system equals (e^2/h) times an integer (the first Chern number). This is the TKNN result.
given: Kubo formula: sigma_{xy} = (e^2/hbar) sum_{n =/= m} integral_BZ [d^2k/(2pi)^2] * 2 Im[<n,k|v_x|m,k><m,k|v_y|n,k>] / (E_m - E_n)^2
Sum over filled band n and empty bands m
assumptions: Fully filled band separated from empty bands by a gap
2D periodic system (Brillouin zone is a torus T^2)
conventions: Berry curvature: Omega_n(k) = -2 Im sum_{m =/= n} <n,k|v_x|m,k><m,k|v_y|n,k> / (E_m - E_n)^2
First Chern number: C_1 = (1/(2pi)) integral_BZ Omega_n(k) d^2k
tasks.expected_answer: sigma_{xy} = (e^2/h) * C_1 where C_1 is the first Chern number (an integer) of the occupied band.
verification_hints: C_1 is an integer because the Berry curvature integrates to 2pi*integer over a closed surface (torus)
For a single filled Landau level: C_1 = 1
The result is topological: sigma_{xy} cannot change under smooth deformations that keep the gap open
Dimensions: [sigma_{xy}] = e^2/h which is the conductance quantum

reference.title: Quantized Hall Conductance in a Two-Dimensional Periodic Potential
